purpose: A grouping wrapper for items inside a Toolbar. Used to collect a related set of actions together — visually or semantically. Rendered by the plugin as a pass-through — parent Toolbar handles the actual layout.
whenToUse: Group related actions inside a Toolbar (view toggles, sort options)
As an alias when the spec generator wants to group semantically
When you need a named container for a subset of toolbar items
For programmatic show/hide of an item group
whenNotToUse: For a single action — put it directly in the Toolbar
For a separate visual toolbar — use a second Toolbar
For unrelated actions — don't force-group them
In place of ToolbarSpacer — that's for spacing, not grouping
doRules: Group items that share a semantic purpose (all filter actions, all view options)
Keep group size to 2–5 items for scannability
Use ToolbarSpacer between groups when visual separation is needed
Support show/hide of the entire group as a unit
dontRules: Do not nest ToolbarItems inside ToolbarItems
Do not use for individual items — use direct Toolbar children
Do not stack groups without separators
Do not use outside a Toolbar

contentGuidance.contentRules: Contains only Toolbar-legal items (Button, IconButton, Select, etc.)

responsiveBehavior.S: May collapse entirely into overflow menu

requirements: role='group' with aria-label describing the group's purpose
Focus order follows visual order within the group
Child items provide their own labels
Group can be reached via a single Tab, then Arrow keys within
